evidence_quotes.quote: unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns
Publishers sell videogames that become unplayable at a later date.

paragraphs.text: Hi, Initiatives like this really make me glad to live in the EU. This initiative is about "unfair commercial practices related to dark patterns". This seems to fit nicely into an extremely unfair commercial practice that video game publishers have been doing more and more. Publishers sell videogames that become unplayable at a later date. This is done in a deliberate way and render all copies of the game unplayable at once. Video games must remain playable after publishers end support. Can you please add a clause to this initiative to address this? 1.4 million people in the EU have signed the European Citizens' Initiative to Stop Destroying Videogames. That initiative and the organizers have more details on this matter. Thank you for your consideration.
